intro: This is the official lexical surface of AccountCcy.com — how the site names monetary truth, currency-state transitions, custody discipline, and audit-facing reporting reality inside enterprise finance. It is not a flat dictionary: grouped clusters show how definitions reinforce doctrine, framework architecture, operational accounting controls, and evidentiary closure.
doctrine_statement:
Precision of language is precision of control.
When treasury, accounting, systems, consolidation, and audit share vocabulary, fragmentation surfaces before it metastasizes into misstatement.
primary_section_title: Enterprise finance collapses unlike meanings into the same ISO symbol unless currency states are named.
primary_section_body: Two balances may both display USD while answering different custody questions — quotation versus obligation, settlement rail versus account assignment, ledger measurement versus reporting translation. AccountCcy publishes both the Currency State Control Framework and the CCY State Chain so those distinctions remain navigable instead of tribal.

The clusters below map thirty-two published defined terms into five conceptual authorities. Each card links to the sovereign term page where doctrine, operational meaning, distinctions, custody questions, and evidence posture are stated without thinning content into slogans.

authority_sections.title: Monetary Truth & Custody
paragraphs: This cluster anchors why AccountCcy treats monetary truth as earned through custody — lineage across sources, postings, and controls — rather than declared through dashboards alone.
Account currency and explicit account-currency control appear here because State 04 is where external monetary movement receives accountable ledger identity inside ERP discipline.

authority_sections.title: Currency State Architecture
paragraphs: Currency is not merely an ISO code floating beside an amount — it is an operational state inside enterprise finance. Naming states exposes configuration fractures before close cycles absorb them.
Linked terms span quotation through ledger custody; the CCY State Chain pillar binds them into the canonical ordered reference.

pillar_cards.label: Framework
pillar_cards.title: The CCY State Chain
pillar_cards.body: Eight ordered institutional currency states from quoted signal through audit-defensible closure — the navigable spine tying doctrine to operational evidence.

authority_sections.title: Enterprise Accounting Control
paragraphs: Functional currency determination, period-end revaluation, translation methodology, and consolidation currency decisions interact inside multi-currency close machines — often silently.
These terms describe mechanisms controllers defend when auditors challenge rate lineage, equity translation adjustments, or group-level elimination narratives.

authority_sections.title: FX, Translation & Reporting Mechanics
paragraphs: This cluster names how monetary values change across measurement, settlement, translation, consolidation, and disclosure—rate sources, remeasurement versus translation discipline, realized versus unrealized FX, and adjustments living in equity or group journals.

It complements foundational FX revaluation, translation, reporting-currency, and consolidation-currency entries by supplying operational mechanics vocabulary CFOs, controllers, treasury, and auditors use when defending lineage—not generic FX commentary.

authority_sections.title: Audit, Evidence & Control Discipline
paragraphs: This cluster defines the language of monetary evidence—trails, reconstruction, state ownership, reporting fidelity, defensibility, and ambiguity risk—so doctrine and framework claims survive independent scrutiny.
Quotation discipline and outward reporting lenses sit alongside audit posture because weak lineage upstream invalidates polished narratives downstream.

control_title: Shared definitions collapse debate cycles into governance cycles.
control_body: When treasury speaks quotation risk while accounting hears ledger truth without naming states, organizations rehearse the same FX drama quarterly. Anchoring vocabulary converts recurring confusion into tractable control maps.
risk_body: Calling every balance "the FX position" or treating reporting currency as interchangeable with account currency erases visibility until close breaks or auditors ask uncomfortable traceability questions.

schema_notes.framework_status: Glossary hub renders thirty-two published term URLs grouped into five conceptual authority clusters.
